# fpcloud_registry_retention_policy (Resource)

Manages an auto-delete retention policy for a project's container registry repos (ADR-038). An hourly sweeper enforces every enabled policy across the project, independent of Terraform — this resource only manages the policy, not when enforcement fires. Deletion is irreversible: keep_last protects the newest N tags, max_age_days deletes tags older than N days, both together apply as AND.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Project-wide default: keep the newest 10 tags, delete anything older than 30 days.
resource "fpcloud_registry_retention_policy" "default" {
  project_id = fpcloud_project.example.id

  keep_last    = 10
  max_age_days = 30
  enabled      = true
}

# A per-repo override: this one repo keeps a longer history.
resource "fpcloud_registry_retention_policy" "releases" {
  project_id = fpcloud_project.example.id
  repo       = "example/releases"

  keep_last    = 50
  max_age_days = 0
  enabled      = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this policy is enforced by the hourly sweeper. Required — an enabled policy needs keep_last and/or max_age_days set to actually delete anything.
- `project_id` (String) The project this policy applies to. Changing it forces a new policy.

### Optional

- `keep_last` (Number) Always keep the newest N tags, regardless of age. 0 = no keep-last floor.
- `max_age_days` (Number) Delete tags older than N days (the newest keep_last tags are always protected). 0 = no age limit.
- `repo` (String) Repository this policy overrides (project-relative name). Empty (the default) is the project-wide default policy, applied to any repo without its own override. Changing it forces a new policy.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the policy was created.
- `id` (String) Retention policy ID.
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the policy was last updated.
